title: mentha × sativa subsp. acutifolia
intro: Mentha × sativa subsp. acutifolia, also known as Japanese mint or prickled mint, is a subspecies of the hybrid mint plant that is primarily native to East Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties, which are attributed to its essential oils and flavonoids. The health benefits of Mentha × sativa subsp. acutifolia include its potential to alleviate digestive issues, reduce stress and anxiety, and improve respiratory health. In terms of herbal preparations, it can be consumed as a tea, added to salads, or used as a flavoring agent in various culinary dishes.
